title: QUIC CRT client-side 0-RTT packet-emission attempt architecture
purpose: Describe how the managed client/runtime path admits the first bounded 0-RTT packet-emission attempt from the dormant detached carrier without widening 0-RTT receive handling or anti-replay.
risks: If the 0-RTT packet-protection package reuses the 1-RTT material or API shape, the slice will overclaim supported 1-RTT behavior instead of a distinct early-data attempt.
If the send gate leaks into public API, the attempt floor will widen the promise instead of remaining an internal client/runtime decision.
If receive handling or anti-replay is folded into the same slice, the family becomes too large to prove honestly in one turn.
If fallback on missing prerequisites is not clean, the slice could leave the runtime looking ready when it is not.
design_summary: The managed client/runtime path keeps the dormant detached carrier and the readiness observation as the precondition for the first client-side 0-RTT send attempt. When that precondition is satisfied, the runtime derives a distinct 0-RTT packet-protection package from the carrier and the live transport state, then feeds the protected packet into the existing connection-owned send path. The current 1-RTT send path remains unchanged, and the slice stops at the first send attempt rather than expanding into receive handling or replay control.
alternatives_considered: Add 0-RTT receive handling in the same slice. Rejected because the first honest send-attempt boundary is already large enough and receive/replay is a separate family.
Keep the family as readiness only. Rejected because the repo already has the readiness observation and now needs a real send-attempt boundary.
Promote the attempt floor into public API. Rejected because the slice must remain internal and managed-client only.
edge_cases_and_constraints: The slice stays client-role only and managed client/runtime only.
Missing dormant carrier material or a failed 0-RTT derivation must keep the send gate closed.
The 0-RTT packet-protection package must remain distinct from the current 1-RTT package and must not alias its state.
The first send attempt must not imply 0-RTT receive support, server admission, anti-replay, or key-update support.
The accepted abbreviated resumption boundary from `REQ-QUIC-CRT-0137` and the readiness observation from `REQ-QUIC-CRT-0140` remain unchanged.
data_and_state_considerations: The detached carrier already holds the resumption credential material and the early-data prerequisite material captured by the earlier slices. The new 0-RTT attempt boundary may need a separate internal protection package or encryption-level marker, but it must stay distinct from the owned 1-RTT material and from the public API surface. Missing or invalid prerequisite material must leave the send gate closed.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice is limited to the first client-side 0-RTT packet-emission attempt. It does not add 0-RTT receive handling, anti-replay enforcement, key update, transfer, retry, or any public API widening.
